title: The Urgency of 'The Post' With Screenwriters Liz Hannah and Josh Singer
format: Liz Hannah and Josh Singer on writing 'The Post' together, from Hannah's spec script to Singer joining the set after Spotlight.

segments.heading: In conversation
segments.guest: Liz Hannah and Josh Singer on

films.title: The Post
films.note: Hannah's spec, inspired by Katharine Graham's memoir, sold to Amy Pascal within a year; Spielberg signed on after reading it in February and shot on a sub-12-week schedule to make a 2017 release.

films.title: Spotlight
films.note: Singer's Best Original Screenplay win, the credential that had him "understood to be capable" when Christy Diesel called about joining The Post.

films.title: The Fifth Estate
films.note: Singer's first produced script and first journalism movie, grown out of a spec about George Gershwin that Spielberg beat him to.

films.title: Wonder Woman
films.note: Hannah's example of a movie already showing a woman empowered; the appetite for more of that, she says, is what The Post taps into.

segments.heading: The last great thing they've seen
segments.guest: Liz Hannah and Josh Singer on

films.title: Whiplash
films.note: The film Singer's agent had him watch the morning before he first met Damien Chazelle about First Man; it's why he took the job.

films.title: First Man
films.note: Singer's next produced script, a Neil Armstrong biopic with Chazelle, brought up here about a year before release.
